reasoning: The USDA FSIS recall process is an administrative procedure that requires verifying that all recalled products have been recovered or accounted for and that the firm has taken corrective action. FSIS Directive 8080.1 outlines that termination (the 'Closed' status) only occurs after FSIS determines all reasonable efforts have been made and effectiveness checks are completed. The time from the announcement (April 5, 2025) to July 1, 2025, is only 86 days. Historical data and general administrative timelines for federal agencies indicate that Class II recalls almost never close within a 90-day window, typically remaining 'Active' for 6 months to a year while disposal and recovery audits are finalized. Since the product's shelf life (often months for vacuum-sealed sausages) may still overlap with the target period, FSIS is highly unlikely to move the status to 'Closed' by July 1.
